grants.name: Amber Grant - WomensNet
grants.description: One application makes a woman-owned business eligible for the monthly Amber Grant and related category grants.
grants.notes: Strong fit for a woman-led founder brand with commerce, education, and wellness positioning. Application is short but paid. Best angle: Yrmis' long track record building consumer brands, Mission Heirloom, and All Black Sheep / MyHeadquarters audience education.
grants.nextAction: Submit before April 30, 2026 with a concise founder story and a clear use of funds tied to growth, audience expansion, and productized education.
grants.requiredMaterials: Short business narrative, website URL, what the money funds, proof of women ownership, payment for $15 application fee.
grants.applicationDraft: Yrmis is a longtime founder and operator who has repeatedly built mission-led consumer businesses at the intersection of wellness, education, and commerce. From YRMIS fashion to Mission Heirloom and now MyHeadquarters / All Black Sheep, she turns lived expertise into trusted products and educational content that help people make healthier, more informed choices. Amber Grant funding would support audience growth, product development, and conversion systems for the next stage of this platform.
grants.emailSubject: Amber Grant application draft for Yrmis
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: Amber Grant application draft for Yrmis

Top recommendation for this week. The application is relatively light and fits Yrmis well. Draft angle: emphasize a repeat founder who has built brands in fashion, food, and wellness education, and is now scaling a platform that turns expert knowledge into practical consumer guidance and digital products. Suggested use of funds: audience growth, content production, funnel optimization, and new downloadable guides or offers.

grants.name: IFundWomen Universal Grant
grants.description: Universal application that can match Yrmis to future IFundWomen partner grants.
grants.notes: Very good baseline move because it creates future surface area rather than a one-off submission. Especially useful if Yrmis can be positioned as a women-led business with strong mission, community, and consumer education.
grants.nextAction: Complete the universal application this week and prepare reusable grant answers so future matches are faster.
grants.requiredMaterials: Founder bio, business description, traction, use of funds, impact story, clear category positioning, links to website and socials.
grants.applicationDraft: Yrmis leads a mission-driven business platform that blends education, commerce, and community around healthier consumer choices and thoughtful living. The business is differentiated by decades of founder experience, strong storytelling, and an ability to translate complex wellness and ingredient research into actionable content and products.
grants.emailSubject: IFundWomen universal grant draft for Yrmis
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: IFundWomen universal grant draft for Yrmis

This is one of the highest-leverage actions because a single submission can unlock future partner grants. Recommended framing: a women-led, mission-driven platform that converts deep founder expertise in consumer brands, food, wellness, and education into content, digital products, and commerce. Keep answers reusable so we can recycle them for future applications.

grants.name: Entreprenista Evolve Growth Grant
grants.description: Quarterly grant for women-owned businesses with growth potential and brand momentum.
grants.notes: Worth keeping in the pipeline, but I need stronger confirmation of revenue and the precise operating entity before rating this higher.
grants.nextAction: Confirm revenue eligibility and the primary legal entity that should apply.
grants.requiredMaterials: Revenue range, business registration details, growth story, proof of traction.
grants.applicationDraft: Focus on the founder's repeated ability to identify underserved markets and build commercially credible brands across categories.
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: Entreprenista grant notes

This one stays in the pipeline, but it is not the highest-priority action until we verify revenue and entity fit.

grants.name: Hey Helen Grant - Visionaries
grants.description: Privately funded unrestricted grant for U.S.-based women-owned, mission-driven businesses under $1M revenue.
grants.notes: One of the strongest immediate fits. The official page says it is for women-owned, mission-driven U.S. businesses under $1M revenue and requires three long-form responses. Yrmis has a compelling mission arc across wellness, education, and healthier consumption.
grants.nextAction: Prioritize this week. Draft the three long-form responses and prepare a crisp explanation of mission, traction, and exactly what $10K unlocks over six months.
grants.requiredMaterials: Three long-form responses, women-owned for-profit U.S. business details, annual revenue under $1M, U.S. business bank account, use-of-funds plan.
grants.applicationDraft: Yrmis is building a mission-driven business rooted in healthier living, informed choices, and community education. Across YRMIS, Mission Heirloom, and her current platform work, she has consistently turned insight into real products, memorable brand experiences, and trusted content. The next phase is about scaling this work into a stronger education-and-commerce engine that reaches more people and converts expertise into sustainable revenue.
grants.emailSubject: Hey Helen Grant draft for Yrmis
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: Hey Helen Grant draft for Yrmis

This is the top application to complete first. Suggested core story: Yrmis is a repeat founder with a long record of creating mission-led brands that improve how people eat, shop, and learn. The grant would fund growth systems, content production, and productized education that help more people make healthier decisions. We should explicitly connect past traction from YRMIS and Mission Heirloom to the credibility of the current venture.

grants.name: Intuit QuickBooks x Mailchimp Small Business Hero
grants.description: Nomination-based small business program with judging on community impact and nominator passion.
grants.notes: Potentially strong, but the official FAQ says the owner cannot self-nominate and the nominator cannot be affiliated with the business. This is only actionable if a real outside supporter submits it.
grants.nextAction: Identify a credible non-family advocate who can nominate Yrmis and send them a nomination packet.
grants.requiredMaterials: Third-party nominator, 50-300 word nomination, business website/socials, owner contact info, community impact examples.
grants.applicationDraft: Nomination angle: Yrmis has shown resilience across multiple ventures and consistently builds businesses that educate consumers and improve community wellbeing through healthier, more informed choices.
grants.emailSubject: Nomination packet request for QuickBooks Small Business Hero
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: Nomination packet request for QuickBooks Small Business Hero

This program cannot be self-submitted. The best next step is to ask a trusted external supporter to nominate Yrmis. I recommend preparing a short packet with founder bio, website links, and 3 community-impact proof points so an outside nominator can submit quickly.

grants.name: Black Girl Ventures Pitch
grants.description: Hybrid pitch plus crowdfunding program for revenue-generating, under-resourced women founders.
grants.notes: This is better if Yrmis is comfortable pitching live and meets the under-resourced founder framing. Official site says applicants must be revenue-generating and 51% owned by under-resourced women founders.
grants.nextAction: Decide whether to pursue pitch-based opportunities this cycle. If yes, prepare a 3-minute story and a concise traction deck.
grants.requiredMaterials: Revenue-generating business, ownership confirmation, pitch deck, 3-minute pitch, community-facing growth story.
grants.applicationDraft: Yrmis has repeatedly translated founder insight into commercial traction, from apparel to food and now mission-driven wellness education and commerce. The pitch should stress why this founder is uniquely credible and why the audience should want to back the next chapter.
grants.emailSubject: Black Girl Ventures pitch prep notes
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: Black Girl Ventures pitch prep notes

This is a strong option if you want a pitch-based route. I would only prioritize it after Hey Helen and Amber unless you specifically want visibility plus community-backed capital.

grants.name: Patagonia Grant Program
grants.notes: Low fit unless the active entity has a clearly environmental mission. Keep only as a low-priority watch item.
grants.nextAction: Do not prioritize unless a clearly environmental initiative becomes central.

grants.name: Digital Communities Grant
grants.description: Micro grant placeholder for community-driven educational platforms.
grants.notes: Keep as an internal placeholder until there is a verified live opportunity behind it.
grants.nextAction: Replace with a verified live grant before spending time on it.

grants.name: Accelerate(HER) Fund - Hello Alice
grants.description: Hello Alice and Tiger Global fund for women entrepreneurs building breakthrough technologies or tech-enabled companies.
grants.notes: This is worth adding because MyHeadquarters / All Black Sheep can plausibly be framed as a tech-enabled education and commerce platform. The fit depends on how strongly the application can present the business as platform-led, not just content-led.
grants.nextAction: Clarify the tech-enabled story: platform, audience data loops, digital products, and scalable online education infrastructure.
grants.requiredMaterials: Women-founder story, tech-enabled business framing, demonstrated need for funding, growth milestone plan, website and traction proof.
grants.applicationDraft: Yrmis is building a tech-enabled platform that combines digital education, trusted consumer guidance, and commerce opportunities around healthier living. The business uses content, digital products, and scalable online channels to reach and convert an audience seeking better wellness decisions.
grants.emailDraft:
To: cetel1.elvira@gmail.com
Subject: Accelerate(HER) Fund draft for Yrmis

This is a meaningful upside grant if we can present the current business as a true tech-enabled platform. The application should emphasize scalable online education, digital products, audience development, and a roadmap for platform growth rather than just founder expertise.

grants.name: Women Founders Network Fast Pitch 2026
grants.description: Annual women founder pitch competition with Tech/Tech-enabled and Consumer/CPG/Other tracks. Applications accepted April 1-May 31, 2026.
grants.notes: New find from fresh search. Strong fit if Yrmis positions myheadquarters / All Black Sheep as consumer media, education, commerce, or tech-enabled platform. Requires pitch readiness and likely financial summary.
grants.source: Women Founders Network official site + SparkXYZ application
grants.nextAction: Decide track, assemble pitch deck, founder story, validation metrics from Substack/GA/Apollo/Sendy, and complete application before May 31, 2026.
grants.requiredMaterials: Founder/company profile, track selection, validation/traction, financial summary template, pitch deck or pitch answers, ability to attend October 21, 2026 Fast Pitch event if finalist.
grants.applicationDraft:
Application method: online portal, not email.
Apply here: https://www.womenfoundersnetwork.org/about-fast-pitch
Direct Tech/Tech-enabled application: https://www.sparkxyz.io/applications/1841

Deadline:
May 31, 2026.

Best fit for Yrmis:
Strong fit if we present myheadquarters / All Black Sheep as a consumer media, education, commerce, or tech-enabled platform. The official program has two tracks: Tech/Tech-enabled and Consumer/CPG/Other. Based on the current Obsidian notes, Consumer/CPG/Other may be safer unless we can clearly explain the product as a tech-enabled platform.

What to prepare:
- Track decision: Consumer/CPG/Other vs Tech/Tech-enabled.
- Founder/company profile.
- Validation: Substack audience, GA traffic, Apollo/Sendy outreach, waitlist, sales, surveys, or other customer proof.
- Financial summary template.
- Short pitch deck or pitch-style answers.
- Clear use of funds.
- Confirmation that Yrmis can attend the in-person Fast Pitch event if selected.

Suggested application positioning:
Yrmis Barroeta is a repeat founder building myheadquarters / All Black Sheep into a trusted consumer education and commerce platform. Her work sits at the intersection of wellness, modern business, lifestyle, and community learning. This is not a generic content project; it is a founder-led platform with a clear point of view, existing audience channels, and the potential to turn trust into products, partnerships, and recurring revenue.

Suggested use of funds:
Use the grant to build the next layer of the platform: better audience capture, a sharper content-to-product funnel, production support for educational assets, and tools that turn newsletter/social/community attention into measurable revenue.

Humanized answer starter:
I am building myheadquarters / All Black Sheep because I believe people are overwhelmed by information but still hungry for guidance they can trust. My work turns years of founder experience in fashion, food, wellness, and consumer behavior into practical education, tools, and recommendations. The next milestone is to make that work easier to access, easier to act on, and more financially sustainable. A WFN grant would help me move from founder-led momentum to a more scalable platform with stronger systems, clearer products, and a wider audience.

Important note:
This is high effort but high upside. If we only have time for quick applications this week, prioritize Skip, Galaxy, Amber, and Hey Helen first. If Yrmis wants visibility, mentorship, and pitch practice, this is worth doing.

grants.name: Galaxy Grant - Hidden Star
grants.description: Free, short application for women and minority business owners from Hidden Star / Galaxy of Stars.
grants.notes: New find from fresh search. Very low effort and deadline is April 30, 2026. Fit is good because Yrmis is a woman founder, but likelihood is moderate-low because application is broad and likely high-volume.
grants.nextAction: Submit this quick application before April 30, 2026. Use myheadquarters / All Black Sheep and concise use-of-funds language.
grants.requiredMaterials: Name, email, ZIP code, ethnicity/gender/business status fields, brief business details if requested.
grants.applicationDraft:
Application method: online form, not email.
Apply here: https://galaxyofstars.org/galaxy-grants/

Deadline:
April 30, 2026.

Award:
,500.

Why this is worth doing:
This is a low-effort, broad eligibility grant for women and minority business owners. The official page says it is free to apply and takes about 30 seconds. Because the effort is low, it is worth submitting even though the odds are likely lower than more targeted applications.

What to prepare:
- Name.
- Email.
- ZIP code.
- Ethnicity.
- Gender.
- Business status.
- Optional referral field.
- A short business description in case the form asks for one.

Suggested business description:
myheadquarters / All Black Sheep is a woman-led education and commerce platform helping people make more informed choices around wellness, lifestyle, and business. Through newsletter content, digital channels, and founder-led guidance, Yrmis turns complex consumer and wellness topics into practical, trustworthy recommendations.

Suggested use of funds:
The ,500 grant would support content production, audience growth, website improvements, and the creation of practical digital resources that help the community move from inspiration to action.

Humanized answer starter:
I am building myheadquarters / All Black Sheep to help people feel less overwhelmed and more confident in the choices they make for their health, home, work, and lives. As a repeat founder, I have learned that trust is built through consistency, clarity, and real usefulness. This grant would help me produce stronger educational content and reach more people who need practical, honest guidance.

Priority:
High because it is quick and due April 30.

grants.name: Skip's $10,000 2026 Spring Grants
grants.description: Skip spring grant for U.S.-based entrepreneurs and small business owners age 18+. One open grant plus one member-only grant.
grants.notes: New find from fresh search. Very urgent: due April 17, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Good fit if the application is straightforward. Need to avoid paid-member-only path unless Yrmis already has Skip Plus/Pro.
grants.nextAction: Apply immediately before April 17, 2026. Use the free/open grant path, not the paid member-only grant unless already subscribed.
grants.requiredMaterials: Skip account, basic business profile, required application questions, possibly instant business plan or website/product page if needed.
grants.applicationDraft:
Application method: online portal, not email.
Apply here: https://helloskip.com/dashboard/opportunity/10k-spring-grants

Deadline:
April 17, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

Award:
Two ,000 grants: one open to all eligible applicants, one reserved for Skip Plus or Pro members.

Eligibility:
U.S.-based entrepreneurs and small business owners age 18+.

How to apply:
1. Open the Skip opportunity page.
2. Click Start Applying.
3. Complete all required questions.
4. If needed, use Skip's instant business plan, instant website, or product tools to complete the business profile.
5. Submit before the deadline. The official page says late applications are not accepted.

What to prepare:
- Business name and website.
- Founder bio.
- Short description of myheadquarters / All Black Sheep.
- What the business does.
- Who it serves.
- Why funding is needed now.
- Use of funds.
- Any traction: Substack/newsletter, Google Analytics, Apollo/Sendy outreach, social channels, partnerships, audience growth, or product plans.

Suggested application positioning:
Yrmis is a repeat founder building myheadquarters / All Black Sheep as a practical education and commerce platform for people who want better guidance around wellness, lifestyle, and modern business. The business is early enough that ,000 can make a visible difference, but developed enough to have a clear brand, audience channels, and a founder with real operating experience.

Suggested use of funds:
I would use the grant to strengthen the systems that turn audience trust into sustainable revenue: content production, email capture, analytics, digital product development, and a clearer conversion path from newsletter/social traffic to paid offers.

Humanized answer starter:
I am applying because this is the moment where a relatively small amount of capital can create a meaningful step change. I have the experience, the brand direction, and the audience channels. What I need now is support to turn that foundation into a sharper, more consistent business engine. A ,000 grant would help me produce better educational assets, improve the website and funnel, and build the first repeatable revenue paths for myheadquarters / All Black Sheep.

Priority:
Very high. This is the most urgent new grant because it is due April 17.

grants.name: Enthuse Foundation 2026 Grant Program
grants.description: Grant program for women entrepreneurs with established CPG businesses; grants include digital marketing, business tools, insurance, and planet/purpose categories.
grants.notes: New find from fresh search. Fit depends on whether Yrmis is applying through Mission Heirloom or another established CPG entity, and whether it is her full-time business. If yes, this is a strong specific fit for business tools or digital marketing.
grants.nextAction: Confirm whether the applying entity is an established CPG business and Yrmis full-time job. If yes, apply before April 27, 2026 under Digital Marketing or Business Tools.
grants.requiredMaterials: Proof of woman entrepreneur, established CPG business, U.S. citizenship, full-time business status, use-of-funds plan, written answers/interview readiness.
grants.applicationDraft:
Application method: online application through the Enthuse Foundation grant page, not email.
Apply here: https://www.enthusefoundation.org/grant-program

Deadline:
April 27, 2026.

Award:
Multiple grants from ,500 to ,000, including Digital Marketing, Business Tools, Professional Services, Healthcare, Retirement, Business Insurance, and Planet & Purpose.

Important fit check:
This is only a strong application if Yrmis can apply through an established consumer packaged goods business and the business is her full-time job. The official eligibility is specific: woman entrepreneur, established CPG business, U.S. citizen, clear use of funds, and not a previous Enthuse grant/pitch winner.

Best categories if eligible:
- Digital Marketing: best if the request is for ads, email campaigns, creative assets, or marketing support.
- Business Tools: best if the request is for software, web hosting, inventory, analytics, or operational systems.
- Professional Services: best if the ask is legal, tax, finance, or growth support.

What to prepare:
- Confirm applying entity: Mission Heirloom or another CPG business.
- Confirm full-time status.
- Business website and product proof.
- Revenue or traction.
- Specific grant category.
- Exact use of funds.
- Financial need explanation.
- Strong written answers and interview readiness.

Suggested positioning if eligible:
Yrmis has built consumer-facing brands with a strong mission around better living, better ingredients, and better information. The Enthuse grant should not be framed as general support. It should be framed as a specific business tool or marketing investment that helps an established CPG brand reach customers more efficiently and grow with discipline.

Suggested answer starter:
My business exists because consumers want better options, but they also need education and trust. I have built brands at the intersection of product, story, and healthier choices, and I am now focused on strengthening the systems that help the business grow sustainably. This grant would fund a specific growth need, not a vague wish list: the tools and marketing infrastructure required to reach the right customers, measure what is working, and build a stronger foundation for the next stage.

Priority:
Medium until eligibility is confirmed. Do not spend serious application time until we confirm the CPG/full-time requirements.

grants.name: Breva Thrive Grant
grants.description: Quarterly $5,000 grant for U.S. small businesses generating revenue and creating measurable community impact.
grants.notes: New find from fresh search. Official page says this quarter is currently closed, but the program runs quarterly. Added as a Q3 watch item with July 31, 2026 target. Fit improves if Yrmis can document community impact and revenue.
grants.nextAction: Watch for Q3 application. Prepare community impact proof, revenue status, and use-of-funds tied to accessibility, education, or underserved audiences.
grants.requiredMaterials: Revenue-generating U.S. business, community impact statement, high-need/underserved audience impact if applicable, use-of-funds plan.
grants.applicationDraft:
Application method: online form, not email.
Apply here: https://www.breva.ai/thrive-grant

Award:
,000 quarterly grant.

Timeline:
Breva lists quarterly cycles. Q2 closes April 30, Q3 opens July 1 and closes July 31, Q4 opens October 1 and closes October 31.

Eligibility:
- Business has demonstrated community impact.
- Breva strongly prefers businesses operating for at least one year.
- Business must be generating revenue before applying.
- Strong preference for businesses serving high-need ZIP codes or low-to-moderate income census tracts.
- Business must be formed under U.S. law and operating in the United States.

Fit for Yrmis:
This is not a generic women-founder grant. It is an impact grant. Yrmis should apply only if we can clearly explain the community impact of myheadquarters / All Black Sheep: making trusted wellness/lifestyle/business education more accessible, supporting underserved audiences, creating useful tools, or helping people make healthier and more informed decisions.

What to prepare:
- Revenue status.
- Date business started.
- Community impact proof.
- Audience/community served.
- Whether any high-need ZIP or underserved community connection applies.
- Clear use of funds tied to impact.
- Website and business details.

Suggested use of funds:
The ,000 would fund content production, educational resources, outreach, and digital infrastructure that make trusted guidance more accessible to the community and help the platform serve a larger audience with practical tools.

Suggested answer starter:
myheadquarters / All Black Sheep is built around a simple idea: people deserve guidance that is clear, trustworthy, and useful. The platform helps audiences make more informed choices around wellness, lifestyle, and modern work by translating founder experience and research into practical education. The community impact comes from making that guidance easier to access and easier to act on, especially for people who do not have time, money, or support to sort through conflicting information on their own.

Yrmis Barroeta

Medium. Stronger if we can document revenue and measurable community impact.
